mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in consumer credit outstanding and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which expands corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 150 basis points over any rolling 3-year period, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of capacity-constrained expansion, a month-over-month rise in the industrial production index signals pricing power and margin expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates negatively with forward equity returns in more than 40% of observations during the specified regime, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly proxies for corporate revenue expansion, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward equity returns across all available data, or the predictive R-squared remains below 0.01 in a rolling regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers inflation hedging flows and central bank tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in response to inflation surprises exceeding the threshold in more than 50% of cases, or the signal exhibits no directional consistency over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous non-farm payroll growth above trend and a stable unemployment rate indicate a healthy labor market that sustains consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over any 3-year out-of-sample period, or the cross-sectional predictive power vanishes when controlling for sector rotation factors.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led expansion regime, a monthly housing starts reading above 900 thousand units amplifies the positive impact of real GDP growth on corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward equity returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when housing starts exceed the threshold alongside GDP growth, or the interaction effect reverses sign during recessionary periods.
